items.summary: Create a new React Three Fiber / Three.js game hook `use1v1Lol3DGame.ts` that implements a 3D first-person/third-person 1v1 battle game. The hook must manage: player state (position, HP, current weapon, ammo), AI bot state (position, HP, current weapon, ammo, difficulty), projectile physics, and collision detection in a 3D arena.

Implement four weapons in the game hook, switchable via number keys 1–4: Sniper (1 bullet, slow reload ~2.5s, high damage ~80), Shotgun (5 shots, medium reload ~1.5s, spread pattern, medium damage ~15 per pellet × 6 pellets), Pistol (20 shots, fast reload ~0.8s, medium damage ~25), Machine Gun (25 bullets, medium reload ~1.2s, rapid fire, lower damage ~12 per bullet). Each weapon tracks current ammo and reload state.

Implement four AI difficulty levels (Easy, Medium, Hard, Extreme) that scale by reaction time and accuracy. Easy: 1500ms reaction delay, 40% accuracy. Medium: 800ms reaction, 60% accuracy. Hard: 350ms reaction, 80% accuracy. Extreme: 80ms reaction, 95% accuracy. The AI bot dynamically switches weapons based on distance to player (Sniper at long range, Shotgun at close range, Pistol/Machine Gun at medium range) with weapon switching thresholds scaled by difficulty. Player selects difficulty on a pre-game screen.

Build a 3D arena scene in the page component `OneLolGame.tsx` using React Three Fiber. The arena should include a flat ground plane, perimeter walls, and several cover obstacles (boxes/pillars) scattered around. Use pointer-lock controls or orbit-style WASD movement so the player can navigate the arena in 3D. Render the AI bot as a visible 3D mesh that moves around the arena. Display projectile meshes (small spheres or cylinders) that travel through the scene.

Add an in-game HUD overlaid on the 3D canvas showing: player HP bar, AI bot HP bar, current weapon name and ammo (e.g. 'Pistol 18/20'), reload progress bar when reloading, selected difficulty label, and a crosshair in the center of the screen.

Show a game-over screen when either the player or AI bot reaches 0 HP. Display who won, the match result, and a 'Play Again' button that resets the game (returning to difficulty selection). Integrate score submission using the existing ScoreSubmission component when the player wins.
